inputs.label: standard
inputs.project: BLACKOUT — a podcast about power grid failures, energy infrastructure, and the politics of electricity
inputs.tone: Technical but human. The feeling of a well-reported piece in Wired or Bloomberg Businessweek.
inputs.color: Industrial — steel grey, warning amber, electrical blue accent
inputs.audience: Professionals in energy, policy, and journalism aged 30-55
inputs.references: The visual language of Bloomberg Businessweek features, the photography of Edward Burtynsky, industrial infrastructure
inputs.avoid: Stock photo power lines against sunset, green-energy marketing imagery

inputs.label: european
inputs.project: BORDER STORIES — a documentary series about life in European border towns, from the Finnish-Russian border to Gibraltar to the Polish-Ukrainian frontier
inputs.tone: Observational, patient, respectful of the specific. Not dramatic — the everyday reality of people who live where countries meet.
inputs.color: Muted, desaturated, season-specific — grey winter light in the north, dusty warm light in the south
inputs.audience: Documentary and journalism audiences in Europe aged 25-50
inputs.references: The photography of Josef Koudelka (specifically Exiles), the cinema of Aki Kaurismäki, the visual register of ARTE documentaries
inputs.avoid: Political map graphics, barbed-wire clichés, passport/checkpoint stock imagery
